title: Exact Solution for Non-Hermitian Free Fermions: A Case Study of the XY Chain
authors: Yuguan Li, D. C. Liu, Murray T. Batchelor
abstract: We consider the non-Hermitian XY spin chain with open boundary conditions when the anisotropy parameter is extended to complex values. By analyzing the quasi-Hamiltonian matrix, we demonstrate that the free-fermion structure of the quasi-energy spectrum coincides with that of the Hermitian model and construct the corresponding biorthogonal fermionic basis away from exceptional points (EPs). We make use of an explicit Chebyshev-polynomial representation of the open-boundary eigenvectors in which the quasi-energy $\varepsilon$ is the natural spectral variable. This quasi-energy polynomial form is particularly useful at EPs, because EPs correspond to repeated roots of the same boundary polynomial, making the construction of generalized eigenvectors by $\varepsilon$-differentiation transparent. At EPs, where the quasi-Hamiltonian becomes defective, we derive the Jordan normal form and construct the associated generalized eigenvectors, which yields the correct counting of independent many-body eigenstates. We further show that EPs act as branch points in the complex anisotropy plane, leading to the characteristic permutation of eigenenergies and eigenstates upon encirclement. The branch-cut structure of the biorthogonal eigenstates provides direct evidence for the exchange of eigenstates when an EP is encircled. These results provide an analytically controlled many-body platform for studying EP physics and non-Hermitian topology beyond momentum-space descriptions.
summary: This paper provides an exact analytical solution for the non-Hermitian XY spin chain, specifically detailing the behavior of the system at Exceptional Points. It demonstrates how the energy spectrum and eigenstates undergo permutation when encircling these points in the complex parameter plane. The work establishes a robust framework for studying non-Hermitian topology using a real-space, many-body approach.
main_problem: The paper seeks to find an exact solution for the non-Hermitian XY spin chain with open boundary conditions, specifically addressing the algebraic structure and eigenvector construction at Exceptional Points (EPs).
main_result: The authors derive a complete analytical solution including the Jordan normal form at EPs, demonstrating that EPs act as branch points that cause the permutation of eigenenergies and eigenstates upon encirclement.
method: The study employs a quasi-Hamiltonian analysis using Chebyshev polynomials to represent eigenvectors and a Jordan normal form approach to handle the defective nature of the Hamiltonian at EPs.
model_or_system: A non-Hermitian XY spin chain with open boundary conditions, where the anisotropy parameter gamma is extended to complex values, mapped to a quadratic spinless-fermion problem via Jordan-Wigner transformation.
key_observables: Quasi-energy spectrum, many-body energy spectrum, biorthogonal overlap (langle L|R rangle), and the branch-cut structure of eigenstates.
important_parameters: Complex anisotropy parameter gamma, system size L, and quasi-energy epsilon.
main_assumptions: The study focuses on open boundary conditions; the Hamiltonian is assumed to be complex-symmetric, allowing the left eigenvector to be the transpose of the right eigenvector.
figures_summary: Figure 1 and 2 show the magnitude and cusp structure of the biorthogonal overlap in the complex gamma plane; Figure 3 and 4 visualize the branch-cut structure and Riemann surface of the overlap.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the non-Hermitian XY model, develops a Chebyshev-polynomial representation for eigenvectors, analyzes the system away from and at Exceptional Points using Jordan normal forms, and concludes with a topological analysis of the complex parameter plane.
why_interesting: It provides an analytically controlled many-body platform for studying non-Hermitian topology and the physics of Exceptional Points in real space, which is highly relevant to open quantum systems and many-body dynamics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Plot of the inner product |⟨L|R⟩| as a function of the complex parameter γ for L = 4. Lines are calculated using the procedure described in the text, and dots are obtained by exact diagonalization.
FIG. 2. Plot of |⟨L|R⟩| as a function of complex γ for L = 4 in the γ plane.
FIG. 3. Plots of the real and imaginary parts of ⟨L|R⟩as functions of γ for L = 4 in the complex γ plane.

FIG. 4. Real and imaginary parts of the Riemann surface of ⟨L|R⟩for the pair of degenerating eigenvectors that exchange when the EP is encircled in the complex γ plane. The black line marks the branch cut: crossing it takes the system from one sheet to the other and exchanges the two eigenstates.
